body:
LOW-MEDIUM, test reliability / render-path robustness. Observed 2026-07-24 while landing T74: on a FULL `vitest run`, `renderers.test.ts > publishes a renderer and serves the route as HTML` and `render-sandbox.test.ts > the happy path is unchanged ... (rail b)` each returned 500 instead of 200 — ONCE. Both passed on re-run and pass 24/24 in isolation. So it is a load flake, not a broken renderer: the render path spawns a bounded worker (§23.9) to execute a bundle, and under full-suite CPU contention the worker's own deadline (or spawn) is missed, surfacing as a 500 from `serveRoute`.

WHY IT MATTERS. A 500-under-contention is indistinguishable, to a caller, from a genuinely broken renderer — and the §23.9 bounded-worker rail exists precisely to keep a bad bundle from wedging the host, so a false 500 on a GOOD bundle erodes the exact guarantee that rail asserts. It also trains a red-bar reader to re-run instead of investigate (the flake discipline's named harm).

MEASURE FIRST (flake discipline). Reproduce under load (e.g. run the full suite repeatedly, or pin CPU) and confirm: happy-path 500s only under contention, never in isolation; on the branch AND on main. Attribute to the worker's timeout/spawn, not the bundle.

THE FIX (determine which, do not widen the assertion). Either (a) the worker's happy-path render deadline is too tight to survive contention and should be raised / made wall-clock-fair while KEEPING the bad-bundle timeout rail meaningful (the two deadlines may need to differ: a real render gets generous time, a wedged bundle is still killed), or (b) the test spawns workers with insufficient isolation and should serialize the render-worker tests. Prefer (a) if the production path is genuinely fragile under load — that is a real robustness bug, not just a test issue.

Evidence: PR #188 description (the timeout-flake fix that surfaced this); the render worker lives in src/gateway/render-worker.ts and is invoked from serveRoute in src/gateway/renderers.ts.

--- UPDATE 2026-07-24 (PR #190) ---
Diagnosed: the render worker's hard budget is RENDER_TIMEOUT_MS = 500ms and INCLUDES worker_threads spawn. Under full-suite CPU contention a legitimately-good render overruns it and folds to 500. It is a TEST-contention artifact (many files hammering CPU in parallel), not a production condition (one render per request, warm process).
#190 fixed the OBSERVED case — the happy-path rail ('a normal bundle still renders 200') now boots with a 10s clock, matching the file's existing memory-rail pattern (observe correct render without racing the timeout bound; the hang + 1ms rails still prove the bound). REMAINING: (1) the hang rail serves a concurrent `ok` at the default 500ms clock (render-sandbox.test.ts ~line 74) — same latent race, not yet observed failing; if it flakes, give that gateway a longer budget (hang still times out, just later) and drop the '500' from the test name. (2) DESIGN Q (Myk): should the PRODUCTION RENDER_TIMEOUT_MS itself be raised, or made to exclude spawn time (start the render budget at worker `online`, which the memory rail comment says was already partly done)? 500ms incl. spawn may be too tight for a real render under real load, not just in tests. If production is fine at 500ms, this ticket is just test hygiene and closes with (1).

--- RESOLVED 2026-07-24 (Myk + code reading) ---
Myk's question — hot-loaded at query time, or spawned-once-stays-hot? NEITHER, and the answer closes the design half: the bundle SOURCE is pre-loaded at mount (prepareRoute), but v1 spawns a fresh worker_threads Worker PER RENDER. The clock already accounts for that honestly: TWO windows — construction arms a 500ms SPAWN bound, and worker `online` re-arms a fresh 500ms RENDER bound — so spawn never eats the render budget. On a warm production process spawn is single-digit ms; 500ms is comfortable. The flakes were a TEST artifact (a full suite starving spawn itself past its window). Production RENDER_TIMEOUT_MS stays 500. If render latency ever matters, the noted follow-on is a small warm worker pool, not a longer clock.
Test hygiene DONE: #190 (render-sandbox happy path) and #193 (renderers.test.ts, whole file) both boot good-bundle rails with a 10s clock; the timeout bound stays proven by the hang rail (default clock) and the 1ms knob rail. Remaining: nothing active — if render-sandbox's hang-rail concurrent `ok` render (default clock) is ever OBSERVED flaking, give that gateway the same generous clock and drop the '500' from the test name.
title: Render worker starves under full-suite CPU contention — a 500 that only appears at load
